title: elaeocarpus cruciatus
intro: Elaeocarpus cruciatus, also known as the 'Celtis' or 'Nasua' tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus cruciatus include relief from fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
